description: Demo install regressed monorepo typecheck — apps/atlas-app skeleton install upgraded TypeScript via vite peer-deps; yarn test:typecheck now fails monorepo-wide on TS5107 (moduleResolution=node10 deprecated). Expected: adding a demo app into an existing monorepo slot is safe. Got: peer-dep resolution upgraded root TypeScript version, breaking all packages.
resolution: unresolved — three candidates: ignoreDeprecations:5.0 in tsconfig.base.json (fastest, punts deprecation); pin TS in root yarn.lock (blocks future vite bumps); migrate to moduleResolution:bundler (correct, riskier). Tracked: atlasdraw-ca94

description: Excalidraw v0.18 viewBackgroundColor is an AppState field, not a top-level prop — expected: <Excalidraw viewBackgroundColor='transparent'/> silently accepted (TypeScript permits unknown props on loose ExcalidrawProps); got: prop silently ignored, canvas painted opaque white (#ffffff default). AppState fields (viewBackgroundColor, gridSize, theme, scrollModeEnabled) MUST go via initialData.appState, not as direct JSX props. ExcalidrawProps type at types.ts:570 is loose enough to accept unknown props without compile error.
resolution: Pass via initialData.appState: <Excalidraw initialData={{ appState: { viewBackgroundColor: 'transparent' } }} />. Rule: before using any Excalidraw JSX prop, grep ExcalidrawProps interface at code/packages/excalidraw/types.ts:570 to confirm it exists there — if not found, it is an AppState field and belongs in initialData.appState.

description: Excalidraw v0.18 customTools prop does not exist — expected: <Excalidraw customTools={...}/> accepted by TypeScript and registered tool in Excalidraw toolbar; got: ExcalidrawProps has no customTools field; prop silently ignored; no runtime error; tool integration non-functional. Third plan-literal-divergence instance (after viewBackgroundColor Wave 3a, newElementWith Wave 3b). TypeScript does not catch it because ExcalidrawProps is loose.
resolution: Atlas-side dispatch via overlay (mx-682f8a): useAtlasdrawTool + .atlasToolOverlay div + seedToElement bridge. PinTool ships without touching Excalidraw tool system. Convention mx-145cd3 codifies the grep-first rule that caught this before Worker 14 dispatch.

description: Parallel subagent dispatch timeouts during Phase 3 Wave 1 — multiple agents (10+) failed with timeout after preflight warning 'No permission mode set.' Agents spawned without explicit permission mode defaulted to prompting for every tool call, timing out before completing work. Root cause: dispatch did not set mode parameter on Agent tool calls. Same signature repeated across 10+ agents in a single wave.
resolution: Set mode: 'auto' or mode: 'bypassPermissions' on Agent dispatches. Convention: all Phase 3+ dispatches must include explicit mode parameter. Detection: gate-enforcer should flag Agent calls missing mode field in parallel dispatch context.

description: Anti-pattern detector pipeline jam — seed atlasdraw-8a40 (silent-catch, 55 findings) is needs-triage, blocking 6 child seeds (catch-all, fire-and-forget, console-only-error, todo-density, untested-churn, impact-scope). The 5 atlas-owned findings from the original detector run remain untriaged. Root cause: global detector did not consult project's local scope file before filing, creating findings that may not apply to this project's conventions.
resolution: Triage atlasdraw-8a40 first (unblocks 6 children). For future detector runs: pass project scope file as input. Consider scoped rule narrowing detector to only flag patterns violated in this project's own code (not generic anti-patterns).

description: Failure domain declared in mulch.config.yaml but failure.jsonl missing — 11 failure-type records orphaned inside architecture.jsonl instead of living in their own domain. infra gap: config declares the domain, but no file was ever created. Causes: (1) failures filed to wrong domain by convention drift, (2) foxhound searches for failure patterns miss records because they're in architecture domain, (3) cross-referencing with seeds failure issues is unreliable.
resolution: Created failure.jsonl, relocated 3 confirmed failure records from architecture.jsonl. Remaining records in architecture.jsonl checked — only 3 were true failure type. Convention: failures go to failure domain, not architecture. Gate: ml validate should flag type-in-wrong-domain.
